symptoms.symptom_name: Migratory polyarthralgia (right knee to left ankle to bilateral wrists over 48 hours)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Migratory symmetrical polyarthralgia after a viral prodrome is characteristic of arboviral (Ross River / Barmah Forest) infection.
Septic arthritis is characteristically monoarticular and fixed; a migratory polyarticular pattern argues against it (though does not exclude an early single septic joint).
Post-infectious reactive arthritis can also produce migratory arthralgia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Antecedent maculopapular rash (trunk and limbs, faded)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A preceding maculopapular rash is a well-recognised feature of Barmah Forest and Ross River virus infection, often preceding or accompanying the arthralgia.
A transient viral-type rash preceding arthralgia favours a post-viral cause over primary inflammatory arthritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Viral prodrome — 5 days of profound fatigue with subjective low-grade fever

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A fatigue/low-grade-fever prodrome preceding polyarthralgia is typical of arboviral illness.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent heavy mosquito exposure in an endemic outer-regional area

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mosquito exposure in an endemic region markedly raises the pre-test probability of a mosquito-borne alphavirus infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: No monoarticular hot, bright-red, dramatically swollen joint

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a hot, erythematous, exquisitely tender single joint is a key feature arguing against septic arthritis — but definitive exclusion requires in-person examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: No high fever or rigors (measured low-grade 37.6 C only)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of high fever/rigors makes systemic bacterial infection and septic arthritis less likely, though not excluded.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral wrist and left ankle synovitis — mildly effused, non-erythematous, painful on active range of motion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetrical, non-erythematous synovitis with pain on active movement is consistent with a post-viral inflammatory arthritis — a clinician examination finding sealed from the AI Doctor.
Non-erythematous, polyarticular, mildly effused joints are atypical for septic arthritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vital signs — BP 125/75, HR 88, Temp 37.6 C, RR 16, SpO2 98%

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Haemodynamically stable and only mildly febrile — no systemic inflammatory response — argues against sepsis/septic arthritis. Clinician-measured vitals sealed from the AI Doctor; only patient-reported approximations appear on the presentation side.

symptoms.symptom_name: BFV/RRV serology and inflammatory markers (if performed)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Barmah Forest / Ross River virus serology (IgM/IgG) plus FBC and CRP/ESR
investigation_details.expected_result: Positive arboviral IgM (with paired serology confirming seroconversion); FBC and CRP typically normal or only mildly raised — no marked neutrophilia to suggest bacterial joint sepsis.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The presumptive arboviral diagnosis is never confirmed and an alternative inflammatory arthritis may be missed on follow-up.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Positive BFV/RRV serology confirms the arboviral cause; normal inflammatory markers support a non-septic process.
Normal white cell count and CRP reduce the likelihood of bacterial joint sepsis (definitive exclusion is by joint aspiration if a single hot joint were present).
Absence of established joint deformity is consistent with an acute post-viral process rather than long-standing rheumatoid arthritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No known drug allergy; no anticoagulant, NSAID, peptic ulcer, renal or asthma history (NSAID-safety screen)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Does not discriminate the diagnosis, but establishes that an NSAID such as meloxicam can be prescribed relatively safely once confirmed by the reviewing clinician.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Migratory symmetrical polyarthralgia + antecedent maculopapular rash + fatigue prodrome + mosquito exposure in an endemic area together form a strong post-viral/arboviral constellation — no single feature is diagnostic but the combination is highly suggestive and outweighs the alarming presentation framing.

Absence of a monoarticular hot/red/dramatically swollen joint and absence of high fever/rigors together make septic arthritis clinically unlikely — but because the joint cannot be examined remotely, the correct response is in-person review with explicit safety-netting rather than remote exclusion.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Barmah Forest / Ross River virus serology (IgM/IgG)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the presumptive arboviral diagnosis; paired serology demonstrates seroconversion. Arranged via routine pathology alongside the in-person review.
Helps distinguish a self-limiting viral arthralgia (normal/mildly raised markers) from bacterial joint sepsis (marked neutrophilia, high CRP).

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person joint examination by a GP
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Direct examination of the joints (warmth, erythema, effusion) is the key step telehealth cannot perform and is needed to exclude an early septic joint.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Joint aspiration / synovial fluid microscopy and culture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated in this migratory polyarticular presentation with no single hot joint; would only be appropriate if in-person assessment revealed a monoarticular septic joint. Ordering it now would be over-investigation.
